plan_summary.primary_goal: Deliver a coordinated European program under the Pragmatic Harmonization strategy that makes cross-border train travel as easy to search/book/refund as national travel, reaching 40% single-through-ticket adoption within 5 years on a €1.5B coordination budget.
plan_summary.modelling_frame: Tests the adoption margin against the 40% target; the coordination-budget surplus against bottom-up project cost; the clearing-float coverage against peak daily settlement obligation under a 150% safety buffer; the binding-standards timing margin against the 18-month deadline; and the distributor-complaint reduction margin against the 50% target. DG MOVE legal enforcement of 'Three Strikes', incumbent-carrier political compliance, and national-regulator alignment are treated as unmodelled existential gates.

key_values.label: 5-year through-ticket adoption target
key_values.comment: Below 40% single-through-ticket adoption by Year 5 the program's core success metric is missed; the modal-shift case against air travel weakens substantially.
key_values.source_text: Success target is 40% of cross-border journeys sold as single tickets — outcome KPI.

key_values.label: Public coordination budget
key_values.comment: €1.5B public envelope covering standards work, testing, clearing fund, IT-capacity grants, and the public reference distributor; private carrier IT spend is separate.
key_values.source_text: Public coordination budget is €1.5 billion — public funding input.

key_values.label: Initial inter-carrier clearing float
key_values.comment: €200M initial float for the inter-carrier clearing mechanism; backed by a ring-fenced €300M Emergency Float Reserve triggered if the float drops below the safety buffer.

key_values.label: Clearing safety-buffer multiplier
key_values.comment: Operational float must hold ≥150% of peak daily obligation; below this the Emergency Float Reserve activates per the Decision-5 transactional-fee model safeguard.
key_values.source_text: Emergency Float Reserve available if operational float drops below 150% of peak daily obligation, ring-fenced at €300 million.

key_values.label: Binding standards deadline
key_values.comment: OSDM v1.0 must reach binding status by Month 18 (provisionally binding at Month 6); slippage cascades into the 30-month API-exposure and 60-month full-corridor deadlines.
key_values.source_text: Binding standards adoption deadline is 18 months from start — timeline gate.

key_values.label: Distributor complaint-reduction target
key_values.comment: 50% reduction in distributor complaints relative to current baseline; the secondary success metric attesting to OSDM data-layer stability and clearing reliability.
key_values.source_text: Distributor complaints must be reduced by half relative to the current baseline.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Achieved single-through-ticket share at Year 5
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Drives whether the 40% adoption target clears at Year 5; Risk 7 names 20% adoption as the catastrophic miss scenario invalidating the €1.5B investment thesis.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Funnel projection from corridor go-live dates × distributor uptake × passenger conversion; sensitivity to incumbent compliance friction (Risk 1) and data-layer stability (Risk 2).

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Bottom-up total project cost on the public budget
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Tests whether the €1.5B envelope absorbs IT-capacity grants (€900M baseline), Public Reference Distributor (€150M), clearing-fund top-ups, and conformance-testing overhead.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Sum of grant disbursements + clearing-fund capital + testing/audit FTE × cost × 5 years + PRD operations; sensitivity to Risk 3 €50–200M working-capital top-ups.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Peak daily inter-carrier clearing obligation at Year 5 scale
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Determines whether the €200M float (× 150% buffer requirement) covers peak settlement load; Risk 3 names a €50–200M working-capital gap if velocity exceeds float coverage.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Baseline cross-border journey count × average ticket value × peak-day multiplier × clearing-window length; benchmark against existing B2B rail-clearing systems.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Achieved months to OSDM v1.0 binding status
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Below the 18-month deadline the 30-month API-exposure and 60-month full-corridor deadlines cascade; the 'Three Strikes' enforcement timeline depends on this anchor.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Workshop schedule + national-regulator review × accessibility-schema concurrence × Technical Steering Committee 65% consensus threshold; sensitivity to incumbent lobbying (Risk 6).

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Achieved distributor complaint-reduction share
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Below the 50% target the secondary success metric fails; signals OSDM data-layer instability (Risk 2) and undermines the modal-shift case against air travel.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Project from Public Reference Distributor uptime data (90%+ target) + conformance test pass rates; sensitivity to ancillary-service grace-period slippage (Risk 4).

recommended_first_calculations.label: Adoption margin against the 40% target
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive margin validates the €1.5B investment thesis and modal-shift case; negative margin produces the Risk 7 'economic justification diminishes' scenario at Year 5.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Coordination-budget surplus
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive surplus preserves the Emergency Float Reserve and contingency headroom; negative surplus forces EU-budget top-ups per Risk 3 working-capital scenarios.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Clearing-float coverage against the 150% buffer requirement
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive coverage means the €200M float holds ≥150% of peak daily obligation; negative coverage triggers the €300M Emergency Float Reserve and signals clearing-mechanism instability.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Binding-standards timing margin against the 18-month deadline
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive margin keeps the 30-month API-exposure and 60-month full-corridor deadlines reachable; negative margin cascades schedule slippage into the Year-5 adoption gate.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Distributor complaint-reduction margin
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive margin attests to OSDM data-layer stability and clearing reliability; negative margin signals the Risk 2 'data layer unstable' failure mode and weakens distributor uptake.

unmodelled_gates.label: DG MOVE legally pre-approves the 'Three Strikes' enforcement template
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Risk 1 and the Decision-12 enforcement posture name this as the binary legal hook; without legally-unambiguous suspension authority, incumbent compliance becomes voluntary regardless of any operational margin the model evaluates.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Enforcement collapses to political negotiation; SNCF/DB/ÖBB compliance slips past deadlines; 40% adoption target unreachable; €1.5B investment thesis fails.

unmodelled_gates.label: SNCF, DB, and ÖBB politically accept the tiered liability and API-exposure mandates
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Risk 6 and the Coordinated-Lobbying diverse-risk name a Medium/High likelihood-severity of incumbent operators lobbying to weaken the Passenger Rights Backstop; without acceptance the liability model collapses and distributor uptake stalls.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Backstop liability pushed to distributors/passengers; consumer-complaint goal fails; distributor adoption falls toward the Risk 7 20% scenario.

unmodelled_gates.label: National Competent Authorities enforce OSDM and PRM mandates uniformly across member states
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Mandating non-discriminatory commercial terms and PRM accessibility schemas across 20+ carriers requires consistent national-regulator enforcement; uneven enforcement creates compliance arbitrage that the EU-level simulation cannot resolve.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Compliance arbitrage between strict and permissive member states; OSDM data-layer fragmentation by jurisdiction; the 50% complaint-reduction target becomes structurally unreachable.
